mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean direction in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recessionary pressure, triggering risk-off portfolio rebalancing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 7.0, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day returns during late-cycle expansions, or the mechanism reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 500 thousand signal robust economic momentum and consumer spending capacity, driving immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 5-day returns in >50% of months where PAYEMS rises above 500 thousand, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation combined with persistently low federal funds rates signals negative real yields and impending monetary tightening, triggering valuation compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts positive 60-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects underlying economic expansion and corporate profit generation, providing a fundamental floor that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals acute supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances following a >1.0% INDPRO decline, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of stable monetary policy.
